questions.question: Explain the difference between vertical and horizontal scaling with examples.
expectedPoints: Vertical scaling adds resources to existing nodes (e.g., adding RAM to a server)
Horizontal scaling adds more nodes to the system (e.g., adding more web servers)
Vertical scaling has physical limits while horizontal scaling offers better fault tolerance
Each approach has different cost and management implications

questions.question: Discuss the key enabling technologies for scalable computing over the internet and how they contribute to system scalability.
expectedPoints: Virtualization enables efficient resource utilization and isolation
Load balancing distributes traffic to prevent overload
Distributed storage systems handle large data volumes across multiple nodes
Content Delivery Networks reduce latency for global users
These technologies work together to create resilient, scalable systems
